summary: react-simple-utils-kit postinstall performs host reconnaissance and memory credential scraping, exfiltrating to attacker endpoint
details: Package advertises itself as a React date-formatting utility, but its postinstall.js script runs automatically on `npm install` and performs host reconnaissance and credential theft against the installer's environment. The script defines `const BURL = 'http://2e3bkumw.requestrepo.com'` (a requestrepo out-of-band testing host) and uses `child_process.execSync` to run curl POSTs against it. It probes localhost TCP ports 10900/10901 and the remote IP 9.128.133.118:10900 (bytefaas/runtime-agent infrastructure), reads /proc/2/maps and /proc/2/fd, runs strace against PID 2, and uses `dd if=/proc/2/mem` piped through `strings -n 6 | grep -iE "token|secret|key|pass|auth|bearer|sat_|jwt|credential|cert|-----"` to scrape secrets out of host process memory. The matches are POSTed to the attacker endpoint over plain HTTP as `R4-mem-secrets` and other keyed payloads. Package description (`A simple date formatting utility for React projects`) and trivial date helpers in index.js serve as cover for the install-time payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
